title: helmet({ noSniff: false }) — X-Content-Type-Options is off, allowing MIME-sniffing attacks

params.passDetail: helmet keeps noSniff on (noSniff is not false) — the X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff header stops browsers from MIME-sniffing responses.
params.failDetail: helmet is configured with noSniff: false, removing the X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff header. Browsers may now MIME-sniff a response and execute a user-uploaded file (or a text/plain endpoint reflecting user input) as HTML/JavaScript — turning a benign-looking upload or API into stored XSS. Leave noSniff enabled (helmet's default).
params.absentCallDetail: No helmet() call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: helmet() does not set noSniff; the default (nosniff enabled) applies.

provenance.note: noSniff: false is set to serve a file with a loose content type and left in globally, reopening MIME-sniffing XSS on user-controlled responses. Nothing visibly breaks, so it ships.
